slides.title: PQ/T Hybrid Composite Signatures for JOSE and COSE
BBS and Modular Sub-proofs with JSON Web Proofs
Long-Term Validation for JSON Web Signature (LTV-JWS)

polls.text: I support publication of this document
I prefer aligning with LAMPS Composite document?

chat.author: Benson Muite
chat.text: Happy to help

chat.author: Michael P
chat.text: Thanks Benson

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: But that will spell doom for https://www.howmanydayssinceajwtalgnonevuln.com/

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: @sophie, not necessarily, implementations will still do stupid long after we tell then not to.

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: Strictly speaking, if we adopt the draft, whether it is sensible or not is irrelevant because the group fixes it. All that matters is this an interesting question and do we have a realistic chance of solving it.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: TIL: Lazer - https://eprint.iacr.org/2024/1846

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: For short statements, VOLEitH combined with multivariate signatures seem like the most promising path for PQ ZKPs. Something similar to eg. https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/109 but that can actually be improved quite a bit

chat.author: David Waite
chat.text: its all lasers, voles, and mayo trapdoors to me

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: I like the idea of some agility on zkp attribute commitment

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: :heart:  fewer options

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: ^ indeed

chat.author: Brent Zundel
chat.text: Yes please

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: I can't see anyone who is so concerned about security that they do ML-KEM accepting the 512...

chat.author: Ivaylo Petrov
chat.text: To clarify, COSE meeting in on Thursday.

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: Summarized: if you use a bad RNG you're in trouble.

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: @Bas, it is more extreeme though, if you decide to ignore large chunks of the spec and do it your own way, you can be in trouble.

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: (also to be clear, I'm not against that wording. I think it's silly, but if it makes people happy I value people's happiness over not being silly)

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: (Not disagreeing with Deb, but if I'd have to choose between RSA+AES-256 or ML-KEM+3DES, I'm choosing the latter.)

chat.author: Quynh Dang
chat.text: AES was designed between 1995 and 1998 by Belgian cryptographers Joan Daemen and Vincent Rijmen.

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text:
Bas Westerbaan said:

(Not disagreeing with Deb, but if I'd have to choose between RSA+AES-256 or ML-KEM+3DES, I'm choosing the latter.)

highly cursed idea, but I agree

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: SLH-DSA-MD5 over RSA-4096

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: ^ if you like combos like that, as sad as our current hpke suites are.. that is exactly why we tried to get alignment on suites.

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text:
Bas Westerbaan said:

SLH-DSA-MD5 over RSA-4096

I think SLH-DSA-md4 is also technically secure

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text:
@Sophie, arguably, the meet in the middle attack on DES is less of a threat these days as the AI folk have bought up every stick of DRAM, SSD and hard drive in existence.
Unless of course, they are the folk making the attack.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: @PHB so you are saying that excessive token consumption is keeping us safe from attacks on DES? I'm doing my part.

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: @Orie, that or the whole AI thing is just a ruse, ChatGPT really runs on a RaPi somewhere while the monster computer they have built with all the hardware sits waiting to unwind the BitCoin Blockchain... Muahhaahhhaaaa

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: :plus:  If you use it, keep it as-is.

chat.author: Jan Klaußner
chat.text: agree with andrei, its meant as single block

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: I'll just say it here: if we add ASN.1 here, lets make it really really clear, that you should not pull in a full parser / serializer to do it.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: and to be clear, I think we should strive to keep ASN1 out of JOSE in the year of 2026

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: image.png

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: I think not seeing signatures/keys as opqaue blobs is one of the design mistakes that have been plaguing JWK/JWT

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: don't think of it as ASN.1, think of it as the LAMPS thing that has no known structure

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: +1 sophie

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: +1 Sophie

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: For private keys, 128/192/256 bit key and deterministic generation scheme (+ hints if needed)

chat.author: Jan Klaußner
chat.text: The ASN.1 is also ONLY for the EC part, ML-DSA is kept as byte string as defined by NIST and not additionally wrapped

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: If you are doing it yourself, it is because you are writing a crypto library in which case you probably have to support ASN.1 anyway so TLS etc. can use it

chat.author: Hannes Tschofenig
chat.text: Since Claude will write the code, the decision does not really matter much...

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: @Hannes, unless all the Claude tokens are being used to break the blockchain...

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: +1 Hannes... lets help CFRG finish BBS, what can we do to help.

chat.author: Karen O'Donoghue
chat.text: +1 Orie

chat.author: Emil Lundberg
chat.text: Re: JWP claims mapping - I think Christian's approach in BBS-MOD is quite elegant, and we should consider adopting that for JWP

chat.author: Emil Lundberg
chat.text: with the caveat that in practice there'll probably need to be quite strict control on what headers issuers are allowed to issue, to prevent de-anonymization through small variations in the header encoding

chat.author: Brent Zundel
chat.text: Looking at the authors of BBS, are any of them still active?

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: perhaps we can get some additional authors added to BBS in CFRG to pull / push it over the line.

chat.author: Brent Zundel
chat.text: I'm willing to work on it

chat.author: Emil Lundberg
chat.text: I've been contributing to Blind BBS recently, but not yet BBS core

chat.author: Tadahiko Ito
chat.text: feature 1 (archiving document) is achieved by  counter signature.

chat.author: Tadahiko Ito
chat.text: JAdES is European, very rich mechanism, and this one is relatively "lightweight"

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: There is also: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9921/
